description: Speaking style: neutral (acoustic baseline for all style comparisons)
markup: <speak><STYLE set="NEUTRAL">Hello there. I am Jibo, and I am here to help you.</STYLE></speak>
plain_equivalent: Hello there. I am Jibo, and I am here to help you.

expected_acoustic.note: neutral is the reference; centroid_delta_hz_min not set — this entry defines the baseline

description: Speaking style: enthusiastic — used for affirmations and celebratory moments
markup: <speak><STYLE set="ENTHUSIASTIC">Hey! That is absolutely fantastic! Let me look that up for you right now!</STYLE></speak>
plain_equivalent: Hey! That is absolutely fantastic! Let me look that up for you right now!

expected_acoustic.note: enthusiastic shows ~100Hz centroid shift vs neutral in oge.3 measurements

description: Speaking style: confident — used for factual delivery and affirmations
markup: <speak><STYLE set="CONFIDENT">I found exactly what you were looking for. Here it is.</STYLE></speak>
plain_equivalent: I found exactly what you were looking for. Here it is.

expected_acoustic.note: confident sits between neutral and enthusiastic; modest positive centroid shift expected

description: Speaking style: excited — higher energy than enthusiastic, used for surprising reveals
markup: <speak><STYLE set="EXCITED">Oh wow! I just learned something completely new about that!</STYLE></speak>
plain_equivalent: Oh wow! I just learned something completely new about that!

expected_acoustic.note: excited expected to show elevated centroid similar to enthusiastic

description: Speaking style: sheepish — Jibo's error/apology register, slightly subdued
markup: <speak><STYLE set="SHEEPISH">Oops, I am sorry about that. I did not quite get that right.</STYLE></speak>
plain_equivalent: Oops, I am sorry about that. I did not quite get that right.

expected_acoustic.note: sheepish expected to show lower centroid vs neutral; no hard threshold set pending measurement

description: Speaking style: confused — used when Jibo does not understand the request
markup: <speak><STYLE set="CONFUSED">Hmm, I am not sure I understood that. Could you say it again?</STYLE></speak>
plain_equivalent: Hmm, I am not sure I understood that. Could you say it again?

expected_acoustic.note: confused expected to show duration elongation and possibly lower centroid; measure empirically

description: Speaking style: news — flat, authoritative delivery for factual information
markup: <speak><STYLE set="NEWS">The current time is three forty five in the afternoon.</STYLE></speak>
plain_equivalent: The current time is three forty five in the afternoon.

expected_acoustic.note: news style expected to show reduced centroid variance vs enthusiastic; compare duration

description: Pitch down 5 halftones — subdued, lower register
markup: <speak><PITCH halftone="-5">I am thinking about that right now.</PITCH></speak>
plain_equivalent: I am thinking about that right now.

expected_acoustic.note: negative halftone should produce measurably lower centroid; delta is absolute magnitude

description: Pitch up 5 halftones — brighter, more engaged register
markup: <speak><PITCH halftone="+5">I am thinking about that right now.</PITCH></speak>
plain_equivalent: I am thinking about that right now.

expected_acoustic.note: positive halftone should produce measurably higher centroid

description: Duration stretch 0.5 — slows speech by half (daemon inverse-rate semantics)
markup: <speak><DURATION stretch="0.5">Let me take a moment to think about that carefully.</DURATION></speak>
plain_equivalent: Let me take a moment to think about that carefully.

expected_acoustic.note: duration_stretch=0.5 should approximately double output duration vs plain; validate against json_duration_stretch reference

description: Duration stretch 2.0 — speeds speech up (daemon inverse-rate semantics)
markup: <speak><DURATION stretch="2.0">Let me take a moment to think about that carefully.</DURATION></speak>
plain_equivalent: Let me take a moment to think about that carefully.

expected_acoustic.note: duration_stretch=2.0 should approximately halve output duration vs plain; opposite of slow

description: Break 0.5s — short natural pause mid-sentence, e.g. before a reveal
plain_equivalent: Well here is what I found.

expected_acoustic.note: duration should increase by ~0.5s vs plain; confirms break inserts real silence not literal speech

description: Break 1.0s — longer dramatic pause, e.g. thinking beat before answer
markup: <speak>Let me see<BREAK size="1.0"/>ah, yes, I have got it.</speak>
plain_equivalent: Let me see ah, yes, I have got it.

expected_acoustic.note: duration should increase by ~1.0s vs plain

description: Phoneme override — pronunciation correction using Combilex phoneme set
markup: <speak><phoneme ph="b aa n ou">Bono</phoneme> is a musician.</speak>

expected_acoustic.note: byte count should differ from plain if phoneme tag is parsed; listen for pronunciation shift

description: Say-as spell — letter-by-letter pronunciation for abbreviations

expected_acoustic.note: duration should increase vs plain (spelling out letters takes longer than pronouncing the word); listen for j-i-b-o

description: Realistic Jibo greeting: enthusiastic style with a short leading break
markup: <speak><STYLE set="ENTHUSIASTIC"><BREAK size="0.2"/>Hey there! Great to see you again! What can I do for you today?</STYLE></speak>
plain_equivalent: Hey there! Great to see you again! What can I do for you today?

expected_acoustic.note: combined: leading break + enthusiastic style; centroid shift from style should dominate

description: Realistic Jibo confirmation: confident style, plain phrasing, news cadence
markup: <speak><STYLE set="CONFIDENT">Got it. I will take care of that for you right now.</STYLE></speak>
plain_equivalent: Got it. I will take care of that for you right now.

expected_acoustic.note: confident delivery for a task-completion confirmation; modest style shift expected

description: Realistic Jibo apology: sheepish style with a thinking pause
markup: <speak><STYLE set="SHEEPISH">Oh no<BREAK size="0.3"/>I am really sorry about that. Let me try again.</STYLE></speak>
plain_equivalent: Oh no I am really sorry about that. Let me try again.

expected_acoustic.note: sheepish + embedded break; duration should exceed plain by ~0.3s minimum from the break alone

description: Realistic Jibo thinking aloud: confused style, raised pitch, deliberate pace
markup: <speak><STYLE set="CONFUSED"><PITCH halftone="+2">Hmm<BREAK size="0.5"/>I am not quite sure what you mean by that. Could you tell me more?</PITCH></STYLE></speak>
plain_equivalent: Hmm I am not quite sure what you mean by that. Could you tell me more?

expected_acoustic.note: nested PITCH inside CONFUSED style; duration should exceed plain from the break; centroid effect from both style and pitch
